description: HP 8656A synthesized signal generator (100 kHz to 990 MHz; nominally 0.1-990 MHz). Legacy HP-IB (IEEE-488) instrument using two-letter function/units program codes, NOT SCPI. It is strictly listen-only (interface functions SH1, AH1, T0, L2, SR0, RL1, PP0, DC1, DT0, C0): the Signal Generator cannot talk, so it has NO query forms, no status byte, no SRQ, and no identify query. Carrier parameters use a Function-Data-Units sequence (e.g. FR 123.45 MZ); modulation parameters use a Source-Function-Data-Units sequence (e.g. S2 AM 75 PC). Program codes may be upper or lower case. Data is fixed-point; a minus sign is only allowed in Amplitude entries. The data message End Of String (EOS) may be a Line Feed (LF), a bus END (EOI) message, or an internally produced EOS. A Device Clear (DCL/SDC) presets the instrument to 100 MHz, -127.0 dBm, modulation off. The 8656A is the earlier model of the 8656B; it lacks the 8656B's amplitude offset (AO), DC FM (S5), carrier phase increment/decrement (PI/PD), and separate RF on/off (R2/R3) commands.

identity.description: The 8656A is strictly listen-only (HP-IB interface functions include T0 = no talker capability). It cannot talk, has no query forms and no status byte, so there is no remote identification query.

commands.name: frequency
commands.description: Carrier frequency function entry. Follow with a numeric value (up to 9 digits, decimal point allowed, no sign) and a frequency unit code (HZ, KZ, or MZ). Default after Clear: 100 MHz.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: 100 kHz to 990 MHz (10 kHz to 990 MHz with uncalibrated output)

commands.name: amplitude
commands.description: Carrier output amplitude function entry. Follow with a numeric value (up to 9 digits, decimal point and minus sign allowed) and one or more amplitude unit codes. dBm/dBf are logarithmic terminators; DB and EM (EMF) may be combined with a linear unit. Default after Clear: -127.0 dBm.

parameters.name: amplitude
parameters.range: +13 dBm to -127 dBm (+1.00 V to +0.100 uV); DM=dBm, DF=dBf, DB=dB, EM=EMF, VL=Volts, MV=mV, UV=microvolts

commands.name: amplitude_conversion
commands.description: Re-displays the current amplitude in a different unit configuration without changing the actual output. With the Amplitude function selected, send one or more units codes. Does not affect the RF output level.

commands.name: amplitude_step
commands.description: Steps the output amplitude up (UP) or down (DN) by the amplitude increment value once the Amplitude function is selected. Repeated UP/DN codes may be sent over the bus. Use Increment Set (IS) to define the step size.

commands.name: am_depth
commands.description: Amplitude modulation depth function entry. Preceded by a modulation source qualifier (S1/S2/S3/S4). Up to 2 digits, decimal point allowed, no sign. Units in percent (PC or %). Default after Clear: 0%.

parameters.name: depth
parameters.range: 0 to 99 % (+7 dBm output levels and below)

commands.name: am_step
commands.description: Steps the AM depth up (UP) or down (DN) by the AM depth increment value once the AM function is selected. If no source is selected, defaults to internal 1 kHz. Set step size with Increment Set (IS).

commands.name: fm_deviation
commands.description: Frequency modulation peak deviation function entry. Preceded by a modulation source qualifier (S1/S2/S3/S4). Up to 2 digits. Peak deviation up to 99 kHz; resolution 0.1 kHz below 10 kHz, 1 kHz at/above 10 kHz; internal rate 400 Hz or 1 kHz. Default after Clear: 0.0 kHz.

parameters.name: deviation
parameters.range: up to 99 kHz peak deviation; max depends on carrier band

commands.name: fm_step
commands.description: Steps the FM peak deviation up (UP) or down (DN) by the FM peak deviation increment value once the FM function is selected. Set step size with Increment Set (IS).

commands.name: modulation_source_external
commands.description: Selects the external modulation source (front-panel MOD INPUT/OUTPUT) as the source for the AM or FM function. Source qualifier used ahead of an AM/FM function entry.

commands.name: modulation_source_int_400
commands.description: Selects the internal 400 Hz modulation source for the AM or FM function. Only one internal source (400 Hz or 1 kHz) can be active at a time.

commands.name: modulation_source_int_1k
commands.description: Selects the internal 1 kHz modulation source for the AM or FM function. Only one internal source (400 Hz or 1 kHz) can be active at a time.

commands.name: modulation_source_off
commands.description: Turns off a modulation source (or the selected modulation function). To turn off an AM/FM function send the function code then S4 (e.g. AM S4); with multiple sources active, send the specific source qualifier then S4 to disable just that source.

commands.name: standby
commands.description: Programmatically places the instrument in the STBY (standby) state. The front-panel RESET/STBY/ON switch must be set to the ON position for this to take effect.

commands.name: on
commands.description: Programmatically places the instrument in the ON state. The front-panel RESET/STBY/ON switch must be set to the ON position for this to take effect.

commands.name: increment_set
commands.description: Increment Set qualifier. Defines the step (increment) value used by UP/DN for the currently selected function. Sequence is Function IS Data Units. Sending Function IS alone displays (locally) the current increment. AM increment is unitless percent; FM increment is in kHz only.

parameters.name: value
parameters.range: limits same as the selected function

commands.name: store
commands.description: Stores the complete front-panel setup (exclusive of increment settings) into one of ten storage registers (0-9). Follow with a single-digit register number.

commands.name: recall
commands.description: Recalls the stored contents from one of ten storage registers (0-9) and sets the output to those values. Follow with a single-digit register number.

commands.name: sequence
commands.description: Sequentially recalls the stored contents of the storage registers, advancing the sequence counter each time it is sent. Remote sequence operation is also available via an external switch closure.

commands.name: reverse_power_reset
commands.description: Resets the reverse-power-protection circuitry after the source of reverse power applied to the RF OUTPUT has been removed.
